headline: Dashboard live with ICARUS method named and verified; three hypotheses adjudicated below yield floor; HYP-097 refutes your 30% thesis on sub-$3 collar sizing.
what_happened: ICARUS (HYP-093) shipped live: p=0.031, DSR 0.987, but constitutional yield +0.00023%/day falls short of 0.0005 floor—valid signal, insufficient edge.
Dashboard rebuild complete: Sharpe updated to 1.25 (provisional pending TICK-024), shadow P&L calendar merged, Oracle daily brief panel live, skills arsenal indexed.
HYP-097 (collar physics on sub-$3 tier) empirically refutes your ~30% thesis: W* overshoot caps at 0.627/0.798 vs assumed; yield collapsed to 0.0166%/day from 0.05% floor.
HYP-094 and HYP-095 also adjudicated below floor; HYP-091 not significant; HYP-096 operator-withdrawn (unverified rule claim).
what_it_means: You have one named, live method (ICARUS) with statistical teeth but insufficient yield to compound toward the funded-sim gate (you're at day 1/30 shadow). The collar strategy you believed could scale sub-$3 is physically constrained tighter than your model predicted—distribution sizing (RCK, W6) and catalyst-split concentration are the honest next paths. The dashboard now surfaces daily progression and method performance transparently; use it to decide whether to iterate ICARUS or pivot to RCK/W6 before day 30 elapses.
todays_one_thing: Run a measured variance-premium scan on gapper chains (you noted ThetaTerminal cost as the blocker) to validate HYP-096's reopen condition, or commit to distributional sizing (RCK, W6) on ICARUS by EOD to break the yield floor before the next shadow cycle closes.
